steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states that West's generation aligned their style with Abstract Expressionism "instead of with traditionally nonrepresentational forms of Indigenous art." Their visual approach follows a European American movement, not Indigenous tradition — so style alone cannot signal Indigenous identity.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — deny the extent to which cultural identity influences an artist's work.: the passage says their style follows Abstract Expressionism, not Indigenous tradition. ✗
  • Choice B — depend not on stylistic details but instead on an awareness of the artist's identity.: since the visual style mirrors Abstract Expressionism, identifying the work as Indigenous must depend on knowing the artist's identity, not stylistic details. ✓
  • Choice C — obscure the Indigenous origins of certain motifs associated with Abstract Expressionism.: does not address why stylistic details fail to mark the work as Indigenous. ✗
  • Choice D — place a greater emphasis on the artist's biography than on the aesthetic merit of the painting.: frames the relationship incorrectly relative to what the passage establishes. ✗
That is the correct answer.
